title: is there a podman command available to set right selinux context on custom directory locations to store and run container images?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind selinux usage question

**Description**
~~~
# podman info
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.0.0-1.git82e8011.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "29"
  MemFree: 725024768
  MemTotal: 24906027008
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-68.dev.git6635b4f.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ef9132178ccc3d2775d4fb51f1e431f30cac1398-dirty
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 2145120256
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: xxx.hostname.xxx
  kernel: 4.20.13-200.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 6h 56m 38.87s (Approximately 0.25 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 2
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/root-home/containers/graphroot --> new location
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /home/root-home/containers/runroot --> new location

~~~

As you can see I changed the location of RunRoot and GraphRoot. Now if I run a container it gave me the following error message
~~~
# podman run -p 5000:5000 -it centos bash

bash: error while loading shared libraries: /lib64/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
~~~

If I disable selinux using "setenforce 0" the container works
~~~
# podman run -p 5000:5000 -it centos bash
[root@17c1ca3e1773 /]#
~~~

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Change the location of graphroot and runroot in storage.conf

2. run podman info

3. start a container

**Describe the results you received:**
~~~
# podman run -p 5000:5000 -it centos bash

bash: error while loading shared libraries: /lib64/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
~~~

If I disable selinux using "setenforce 0" the container runs
~~~
# podman run -p 5000:5000 -it centos bash
[root@17c1ca3e1773 /]#
~~~

**Describe the results you expected:**
~~~
# podman run -p 5000:5000 -it centos bash
[root@17c1ca3e1773 /]#
~~~

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.11.4
Git Commit:    "49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"
Built:         Tue Jan 15 02:08:17 2019
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: '"49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"'
  go version: go1.11.4
  podman version: 1.0.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.0.0-1.git82e8011.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "29"
  MemFree: 205803520
  MemTotal: 24906027008
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-68.dev.git6635b4f.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ef9132178ccc3d2775d4fb51f1e431f30cac1398-dirty
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 2145120256
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: xxxxxxx
  kernel: 4.20.13-200.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 7h 12m 4.84s (Approximately 0.29 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 4
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/root-home/containers/graphroot
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /home/root-home/containers/runroot
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I was able to set the right context and start the container using the following commands. 

~~~
# chcon -Rv --reference=/var/run/containers/storage /home/root-home/containers/runroot
# chcon -Rv --reference=/var/lib/containers/storage /home/root-home/containers/graphroot/
# chcon -Rv --reference=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay /home/root-home/containers/graphroot/overlay/
~~~

Is this the right way to set the contexts or its just a dirty workaround?

Thanks,
Anil

I would do 
semanage fcontext -a -e var/lib/containers /home/root-home/containers
restorecon -R -v /home/root-home/containers
